directions.name: Restore accepted grouped_rows=8 base, then test 8->6 hot-band narrowing
directions.hypothesis: The accepted grouped_rows=8 + reversed row-pair + right-left PTX sweep + one-sync handoff + unroll 2 base is still the best locality shape, and the remaining question is whether a narrower kFixedHotBandPtxGroupedRows value of 6 preserves enough reuse without reintroducing the DRAM growth seen at grouped_rows=4.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Locality loss in the hot-band PTX consumer ordering and row reuse window, not the broader sweep or sync structure.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:right-left PTX sweep and one-sync handoff
directions.risk: If 6 is too narrow, it may recover less reuse than 8 while still missing the DRAM amplification seen with grouped_rows=4; however it is the cheapest remaining locality change on the accepted base.
metrics_to_recheck: warp stall mix around the handoff
directions.stop_condition: Stop if grouped_rows=6 does not improve over 25.934336 ms or if DRAM traffic rises toward the grouped_rows=4 regression.

directions.name: Restore accepted base, then probe a very narrow overlap-recovery tweak
directions.hypothesis: The one-sync handoff likely leaves a small overlap-recovery gap that can be tightened without disturbing the accepted grouped_rows=8 locality pattern, so a minimal tweak behind that handoff may recover a small amount of exposed latency.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Residual consumer-to-producer overlap loss at the handoff boundary rather than the main sweep or row-group structure.
directions.risk: This is a low-probability micro-win; if the overlap window is already saturated, the tweak will add complexity without measurable gain.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the tweak does not move runtime or if it perturbs the accepted base's measured stability.

directions.name: Restore accepted base, then revisit a very local consumer-order closure
directions.hypothesis: A final, very local consumer-order closure may still exist inside the right-left sweep family, but only if it preserves the same sweep structure and avoids reopening the rejected broad-order paths.
directions.expected_bottleneck: A small ordering inefficiency in the consumer sequence inside the right-left sweep family.
directions.risk: This is fragile and easy to overgeneralize into the rejected split/mirrored sweep families, so the search space must stay strictly local.
metrics_to_recheck: instruction count in the consumer closure
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the change escapes the right-left sweep family or fails to improve the measured runtime.

notes: Anchored to run 20260420_091330_bf16_gemm_v1_863f60f at 25.934336 ms. Rejected this round: grouped_rows=16 active path, grouped_rows=4 active path, unroll 1 active path, warmup-order reopen, K32 cadence, extra-live B lookahead, CTA-level B repack, broad shared-memory rewrites, split sweep, and full mirrored sweep.
